# datahub_connection (Resource)

**DataHub ✅ | DataHub Cloud ✅**

Creates and manages a DataHub Connection.

DataHub Connections are named, encrypted credential configurations for data platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Unity Catalog) and are displayed on the DataHub Cloud Integrations page. A connection can be referenced in an ingestion source recipe via the `connection` field (e.g., `connection: urn:li:dataHubConnection:my-conn`).

## Security model

DataHub encrypts the entire connection config blob server-side (AES-GCM-256) before persisting it. The encrypted blob is never returned by the read API. As a result:

- All fields inside the platform config blocks are **WriteOnly**: they are sent to DataHub on create/update but are not stored in Terraform state.
- Only top-level metadata (`name`, `platform`) is drift-detected via the strongly-consistent OpenAPI v3 read path.

**Requires Terraform CLI 1.11 or later** (WriteOnly attribute support).

## Credential rotation

Because all config fields are WriteOnly, Terraform cannot detect per-field drift on its own. To update any connection config (credentials or other settings), increment `config_wo_version` (e.g., from `1` to `2`). Terraform will plan a replacement -- deleting and recreating the connection with the updated config from your current configuration.

## Platform config blocks

Exactly one platform block must be configured. Use the block matching your data platform (`databricks`, `snowflake`, `bigquery`, `dataplex`, `redshift`, `unity_catalog`). For platforms without a dedicated block, use `raw_config` and supply the JSON directly.

### Databricks field reference

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `workspace_url` | Databricks workspace URL (e.g., `https://dbc-xxx.cloud.databricks.com`) |
| `warehouse_id` | SQL Warehouse ID |
| `auth_type` | `PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` or `OAUTH_M2M` |
| `personal_access_token_wo` | PAT credential (required when `auth_type = PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`) |
| `client_id_wo` | OAuth client ID (required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`) |
| `client_secret_wo` | OAuth client secret (required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`) |

### Snowflake field reference

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `account_id` | Snowflake account identifier (e.g., `xy12345.us-east-1`) |
| `username` | Snowflake username (optional when using key-pair auth without a username) |
| `warehouse` | Snowflake warehouse name (optional) |
| `role` | Snowflake role (optional) |
| `auth_type` | `DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR` (password) or `KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR` |
| `password_wo` | Password (used when `auth_type = DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR`) |
| `private_key_wo` | PEM-encoded private key (required when `auth_type = KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR`) |
| `private_key_passphrase_wo` | Private key passphrase (optional, for encrypted private keys) |

### BigQuery field reference

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `private_key_json_wo` | Full service account JSON key file contents (the JSON string from the downloaded key file). The project ID is read from inside the JSON. |

### Dataplex field reference

Identical to the `bigquery` block -- Dataplex uses the same GCP service account credential structure.

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `private_key_json_wo` | Full service account JSON key file contents. |

### Redshift field reference

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `host_port` | Redshift endpoint and port (e.g., `cluster.region.redshift.amazonaws.com:5439`) |
| `database` | Database name |
| `username` | Redshift username |
| `password_wo` | Redshift password |

### Unity Catalog field reference

Identical to the `databricks` block but targets the Unity Catalog platform URN.

## Post-import note

After `terraform import`, only `name` and `platform` are populated from DataHub (the config blob is encrypted and unavailable). You must add a matching platform block in your configuration and set `config_wo_version` before the next apply.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "datahub_connection" "prod_databricks" {
  connection_id     = "prod-databricks"
  name              = "Production Databricks"
  config_wo_version = 1

  databricks {
    workspace_url            = "https://dbc-39f83129-3f92.cloud.databricks.com"
    warehouse_id             = "397626f80afeea92"
    auth_type                = "PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN"
    personal_access_token_wo = var.databricks_pat
  }
}

variable "databricks_pat" {
  description = "Databricks Personal Access Token"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `connection_id` (String) Unique identifier for this connection. Becomes the URN suffix (`urn:li:dataHubConnection:<connection_id>`). Must be URL-safe. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name for the connection, shown in the DataHub Integrations UI.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `bigquery` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a BigQuery connection. See field reference in the resource description. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--bigquery))
- `config_wo_version` (Number) Rotation counter for the connection config. Increment this integer (e.g., `1` -> `2`) to trigger a replacement of the connection with the updated config from your current configuration. The integer itself is arbitrary; only changes to it matter. **Requires Terraform CLI 1.11+.**
- `databricks` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a Databricks connection. See field reference in the resource description. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--databricks))
- `dataplex` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a Google Cloud Dataplex connection. Uses the same GCP service account credential structure as the `bigquery` block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--dataplex))
- `raw_config` (Block, Optional) Generic escape hatch for platforms without a dedicated typed block. Supply the platform identifier and the full config JSON directly. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--raw_config))
- `redshift` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a Redshift connection. See field reference in the resource description. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--redshift))
- `snowflake` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a Snowflake connection. See field reference in the resource description. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--snowflake))
- `unity_catalog` (Block, Optional) Configuration for a Databricks Unity Catalog connection. Fields are identical to the `databricks` block but target the `unity-catalog` platform URN. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--unity_catalog))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `platform` (String) DataHub platform identifier (e.g., `databricks`, `snowflake`). Derived from the configured platform block. Drift-detected: if the platform changes outside Terraform, the next plan will surface it.
- `urn` (String) Full DataHub URN for this connection (e.g., `urn:li:dataHubConnection:prod-databricks`).

<a id="nestedblock--bigquery"></a>
### Nested Schema for `bigquery`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `private_key_json_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Full service account JSON key file contents (the JSON string from the downloaded key file). The project ID is read from inside the JSON; no separate field is needed.


<a id="nestedblock--databricks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `databricks`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `auth_type` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Authentication type: `PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` or `OAUTH_M2M`.
- `client_id_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) OAuth M2M client ID. Required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`.
- `client_secret_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) OAuth M2M client secret. Required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`.
- `personal_access_token_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Personal access token. Required when `auth_type = PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`.
- `warehouse_id` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) SQL Warehouse ID.
- `workspace_url` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Databricks workspace URL (e.g., `https://dbc-39f83129-3f92.cloud.databricks.com`).


<a id="nestedblock--dataplex"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dataplex`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `private_key_json_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Full service account JSON key file contents (the JSON string from the downloaded key file). The project ID is read from inside the JSON.


<a id="nestedblock--raw_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `raw_config`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `config_json_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Full platform config serialized as a JSON string. The schema depends on the platform; consult the DataHub source for the expected fields.
- `platform_urn_suffix` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) DataHub platform identifier (the URN suffix, e.g., `looker`, `tableau`). Used to construct `urn:li:dataPlatform:<platform_urn_suffix>`.


<a id="nestedblock--redshift"></a>
### Nested Schema for `redshift`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `database` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Database name.
- `host_port` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Redshift endpoint and port (e.g., `cluster.region.redshift.amazonaws.com:5439`).
- `password_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Redshift password.
- `username` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Redshift username.


<a id="nestedblock--snowflake"></a>
### Nested Schema for `snowflake`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `account_id` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Snowflake account identifier (e.g., `xy12345.us-east-1`).
- `auth_type` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Authentication type: `DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR` (password-based) or `KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR`.
- `password_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Snowflake password. Used when `auth_type = DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR`.
- `private_key_passphrase_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Private key passphrase for encrypted private keys. Optional.
- `private_key_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) PEM-encoded private key. Required when `auth_type = KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR`.
- `role` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Snowflake role. Defaults to the user's default role when omitted.
- `username` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Snowflake username.
- `warehouse` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Snowflake warehouse name.


<a id="nestedblock--unity_catalog"></a>
### Nested Schema for `unity_catalog`

Optional:

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `auth_type` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Authentication type: `PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` or `OAUTH_M2M`.
- `client_id_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) OAuth M2M client ID. Required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`.
- `client_secret_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) OAuth M2M client secret. Required when `auth_type = OAUTH_M2M`.
- `personal_access_token_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Personal access token. Required when `auth_type = PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`.
- `warehouse_id` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) SQL Warehouse ID.
- `workspace_url` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Databricks workspace URL.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by URN
terraform import datahub_connection.prod_databricks urn:li:dataHubConnection:prod-databricks

# Or import by bare connection ID (provider constructs the URN)
terraform import datahub_connection.prod_databricks prod-databricks

# NOTE: After import, the connection config blob is encrypted at rest and cannot
# be read back from the DataHub API. You must add the appropriate platform block
# to your configuration with the correct credentials and set config_wo_version
# before running terraform apply.
```
